sections.heading: Tour Our Additive Manufacturing Facility
sections.text: Tour Our Additive Manufacturing Facility Take a quick tour through our additive manufacturing facility in North Carolina, one of the largest 3D printing operations in the world, to see how we build high-quality prototypes and fully functional end-use components and assemblies.

sections.heading: Material Properties of 3D Printing Plastics
sections.text: Material Properties of 3D Printing Plastics Our plastic 3D printing material options consist of thermoset resins for quality surface finishes, thermoplastic nylons for functional prototypes, and elastomers like TPU for flexible parts. sections.heading: SLA vs. SLS: Comparing Plastic 3D Printing Technologies
sections.text: SLA vs. SLS: Comparing Plastic 3D Printing Technologies An in-depth comparison of two popular 3D printing technologies: stereolithography (SLA) and selective laser sinter (SLS). Read Blog arrow_right_alt FDM vs. SLA: Comparing Filament and Resin 3D Printers See how these two 3D printing technologies stack up for prototype parts. Understanding the advantages of each will help accelerate design. Read Blog arrow_right_alt MJF vs. SLS: Key Differences and Similarities A look at the key differences between Multi Jet Fusion and selective laser sintering and how to determine the optimal process for your application. sections.heading: Learn More About 3D-Printed Plastics
sections.text: Learn More About 3D-Printed Plastics ABS-like Plastic ABS-like plastic is a good all-purpose 3D printing material that offers impact resistant, water resistance, and overall durability. We offer multiple grades to the meet the demands of several applications. Learn More arrow_right_alt Polycarbonate Polycarbonate is a versatile material choice that builds stiff, strong parts. Multiple polycarbonate materials are available through our stereolithography (SLA) process. Learn More arrow_right_alt Nylon Nylon 3D printing produces parts with excellent mechanical properties suitable for rapid prototyping, functional testing, and end-use production. Learn More arrow_right_alt Polypropylene 3D-print...

sections.heading: High Precision 3D Printing with MicroFine
sections.text: High Precision 3D Printing with MicroFine MicroFine™ is an SLA material only available through Protolabs. The material enables us to achieve features as small as 0.0025 in. In terms of its mechanical properties, the material is fairly durable, rigid, and most similar to an ABS plastic. Smaller features built in MicroFine are best dialed in when parts are around or less than 1 in. x 1 in. x 1 in. Learn More

sections.heading: Our Plastic 3D Printers on Site
sections.text: Our Plastic 3D Printers on Site Our fleet of plastic 3D printers consist of a range of machines to support various material options, build volumes, and resolutions (normal, high, and micro). Stereolithography: 3D Systems Viper, ProJet 6000s, ProJet 7000, iPro8000, and ProX 800s Selective Laser Sintering: sPro60 and sPro140 Multi Jet Fusion: HP Jet Fusion 3D 4210 PolyJet: Objet260, Connex3, Objet350, and Objet500 Connex3 Carbon: M2
